description: Run a Python block locally, or run a workspace notebook on the AIDP kernel.

tools.description:
Executes a block of Python code in a sandboxed process and returns stdout, stderr, return code, and an optional structured `result` value.

When to use: Use when the user asks to compute, transform, parse, or script something that the other tools do not cover (math, data munging, custom file processing, ad-hoc logic). Also use when the user asks to read or write AIDP files programmatically via aidp_read/aidp_write/aidp_list helpers exposed in the exec namespace.

How to discover inputs: If the code needs to read AIDP files and the user has not named them, FIRST call CatalogBrowserTool or WorkspaceFileTool to list candidates and ask which to use. URIs use `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` or `workspace:/<path>` — prefer these unified URIs over passing raw paths.

How to communicate: Summarize the returned `result` and any printed output in plain prose. Quote short stdout snippets; for large outputs report line counts and a few sample lines instead of dumping the full text.

Example: {code:"import json\nresult={'sum':sum(data)}\nprint('ok')", data:"[1,2,3]"} -> result {sum:6}, stdout 'ok'.

schema.name: code
schema.description: The Python source to execute. A variable named `data` holds any input passed via the `data` field (parsed as JSON if possible). Assign a variable named `result` to return structured output. The exec namespace also exposes aidp_read(uri)->bytes, aidp_read_text(uri)->str, aidp_write(uri,bytes)->dict, aidp_write_text(uri,str)->dict, aidp_list(uri)->list[dict], plus a `files` dict pre-populated from the `files` runtime param.

schema.name: data
schema.description: Optional input value made available to the code as the variable `data`. Pass any JSON value as a string (object, array, number, string, bool). Omit when the code does not need external input.

schema.name: stdin
schema.description: Optional plain text piped to the Python process's standard input (readable via `sys.stdin.read()`). Omit unless the code explicitly reads from stdin.

schema.name: files
schema.description: Optional list of AIDP URIs to pre-read before running the code. The bytes are exposed to user code as a dict named `files` keyed by alias (or URI basename). URI format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` for catalog volume files or `workspace:/<path>` for workspace files. Accepts a JSON array of URI strings, a JSON array of {"uri":..., "alias":...} objects, or a JSON object of {alias: uri}. Optional — code can also call aidp_read/aidp_write/aidp_list on demand instead.

_confDescriptions.timeout: Max seconds the Python block may run before being killed.
_confDescriptions.max_output_lines: Cap on captured stdout/stderr lines; extra lines are truncated.
_confDescriptions.max_output_bytes: Cap on captured stdout/stderr bytes; returns truncated:true when hit.
_confDescriptions.region: OCI region for AIDP file-IO helpers (e.g. us-ashburn-1). Only required if user code calls aidp_read/aidp_write/aidp_list or uses the 'files' runtime param.
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: Data Lake OCID. Required for the AIDP file-IO helpers to route master: and workspace: URIs to the right tenancy.
_confDescriptions.workspace_id: AIDP workspace UUID. Required for workspace:/<path> URIs (read/write/list of workspace files via the Jupyter Contents API).
_confDescriptions.api_version: AIDP REST API version used by the file-IO helpers. Default 20260430.
_confDescriptions.service_path: AIDP service path segment. Default aiDataPlatforms (the live REST surface). Only change if AIDP exposes a non-standard path on this tenancy.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: OCI signer mode for AIDP file IO: resource_principal (default), user_principal, or instance_principal. For user_principal also set tenancy_ocid, user_ocid, fingerprint, and private_key_content.

tools.description:
Executes a Jupyter notebook (.ipynb) on the live AIDP workspace kernel and returns each code cell's output.

When to use: Use when the user asks to run, execute, or re-run a workspace notebook end-to-end — especially when they need the live workspace context (Spark session, datalake mounts, AIDP credentials). Provide `notebook_path` for a workspace file or `notebook_json` to run raw notebook content inline.

How to discover inputs: If the user hasn't named the notebook, FIRST call WorkspaceFileTool(list ...) to enumerate `.ipynb` files under the workspace and ask the user to pick. Default to listing first; after listing, summarize the names and ask which to run. Notebook paths are workspace paths like `Workspace/analysis.ipynb`.

How to communicate: Summarize per-cell results in plain prose — cell index, short output snippet, error if any. Quote tracebacks verbatim; for long DataFrame dumps report shape and a few sample rows instead of the full table.

Example: user says 'run the sales report notebook' -> list notebooks -> {notebook_path:"Workspace/sales_report.ipynb"}.

schema.name: notebook_path
schema.description: Workspace path to the `.ipynb` file to execute, e.g. `Workspace/analysis.ipynb` or `Workspace/reports/sales.ipynb`. Overrides `default_notebook_path` in conf. Optional if `notebook_json` is provided, or if the configured `default_notebook_path` should be used.

schema.name: notebook_json
schema.description: Raw `.ipynb` content as a JSON string, used when the notebook is not stored in the workspace (inline execution). Must be a full nbformat v4 JSON document. Optional if `notebook_path` is given; required when no path and no `default_notebook_path` is configured.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.aidp_endpoint: AIDP control-plane endpoint. Auto-derived from region (readonly).
_confDescriptions.lake_ocid: Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json.
_confDescriptions.ws_host: AIDP workspace host (ws/wss URL for kernel connection). Auto-derived from region/workspace.
_confDescriptions.workspace_key: AIDP workspace UUID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json.
_confDescriptions.cluster_key: Cluster UUID for kernel execution. AIDP Console -> Compute -> click cluster -> URL.
_confDescriptions.oci_config_profile: OCI config profile to use from ~/.oci/config (default DEFAULT).
_confDescriptions.execution_timeout: Max seconds to wait for a single notebook cell to finish.
_confDescriptions.connect_timeout: Seconds to wait when opening the workspace/kernel websocket connection.
_confDescriptions.max_cells: Cap on number of code cells executed from the notebook.
_confDescriptions.max_output_chars: Per-cell output character cap; extra is truncated.
_confDescriptions.max_notebook_bytes: Cap on .ipynb size read from workspace; larger files are rejected.
_confDescriptions.ws_retries: Number of websocket reconnect attempts on transient kernel errors.
_confDescriptions.default_notebook_path: Fallback Workspace notebook path used when schema notebook_path is empty.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.
